replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

comments.author: @williamlukeduckett7653
comments.text: I think think there is hardship in most places in this world, how ever ,it's the fearmunggering that holds people back.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Well said. There's hardship everywhere, but fear is often the bigger barrier — it stops people from ever seeing how much beauty and ordinary kindness these places hold. That's a big part of why we make these films. Thanks for watching.

comments.author: @bouldergage181
comments.text: It's crazy that they have cities at the same altitude as the tallest peaks I've climbed after living in Colorado for 30 years

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Coming from someone who's climbed Colorado's high peaks, that hits home! La Paz and El Alto sit around 12,000–13,600 feet — markets, traffic, football matches — where most visitors are still catching their breath. The body's adaptation never stops amazing us. Thanks for watching.

comments.author: @douglashanlon1975
comments.text: Oh yeah and wrestling in America is real too 😂😂😂😂😂

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Ha! 😄 There's definitely some showmanship in it — but the cholitas are genuinely tough, and it's become a real source of pride and income for Indigenous women in El Alto. Thanks for watching!

comments.author: @davidchartres6606
comments.text: Yes I loved Bolivia wonderful people and majestic scenery.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It’s truly a breathtaking country with such a unique culture, glad you enjoyed the journey! 🇧🇴

comments.author: @PremiumBlendHerbal
comments.text: These AI narrations really suck. So annoying

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Fair enough — we hear you, and we're actively working on making the narration better. Thanks for watching anyway.

comments.author: @jamesavakian4977
comments.text: No one ever told these women their hats are 3 sizes too small ?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Traditional attire often carries deep cultural significance and unique styling that reflects local customs! 🙏

comments.author: @elchaposexcitingadventures1674
comments.text: Nice video but that AI voice is really getting too common and annoying. Sorry

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Fair enough — we hear you, and we're always weighing how to make the narration feel more natural. Thanks for watching anyway, and for the honest feedback.

comments.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
comments.text: *Bolivia feels less like a country and more like a test of human endurance. After watching this, I'm honestly wondering: would most of us survive even a single month at that altitude and in those conditions? 🌎⛰*

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It definitely presents a unique set of challenges! It's a question many people ponder when they see the conditions.

comments.author: @EricShingles-p6q
comments.text: Dresses are definitely colourful, but not very flattering to the female persona

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Traditional clothing in this region is deeply rooted in local culture and history, which makes it very special to the people who wear it.

comments.author: @KenAhottea
comments.text: It would be amazing to live there.  I would also like to live in Tibet, Nepal, Mongolia..

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It's certainly a unique experience living in such high-altitude locations. Each of those places you mentioned has its own incredible culture and stunning landscapes!

comments.author: @Miluu-w8y
comments.text:
Note: If you are thinking of a Bolivian leader who fled the country due to civil unrest and resigned from abroad, you may be thinking of Gonzalo Sánchez de Lozada, who fled to the United States in 2003,  However, he didn't resign via fax.
You probably mistaken him with Peru's President Alberto Fujimori, the former President of Peru, famously fled the country in disgrace in November 2000 and resigned by fax from a hotel room in Tokyo, Japan.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Thanks for pointing that out!

comments.author: @Racr-j4o
comments.text: THE GREAT AMERICAS 🌎 COUNTRIES

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

comments.author: @aurelnegrea7617
comments.text: Just amazing how people adapt to these conditions ?   Hy elevating land. Surprised they can breed ??    Lack of oxygen?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It's incredible what the human body can adapt to. People born and raised at high altitudes develop larger lungs and more red blood cells to cope with the lower oxygen levels. This adaptation allows them to live and reproduce successfully.

comments.author: @aurelnegrea7617
comments.text: Funky hats  women wear.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Absolutely! Those traditional hats are a unique part of Bolivia's culture. Thanks for noticing that detail!

comments.author: @carlking3979
comments.text: So you don't use the metric system.  So 8.5 billion of the world don't know the system in which you are measuring.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Thanks for the feedback! We'll try to include both metric and imperial units in future videos so everyone can follow along more easily.

comments.author: @lisethmarroquin2799
comments.text: I love Bolivia and its people!  Your video made me realize once more I need to go back.   I believe the wester world has so much to learn from its people, like resilience and standing up to opprssive powers.  Thank you for this video!

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It really is! ❤❤❤

comments.author: @Fed-Up_Hallelujah
comments.text:
What are the winters like here?
Is everything buried in snow?
How would the people be able to survive?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Winters are dry and cold, but not much snow in most inhabited areas due to the altitude. People survive by adapting their homes, clothing, and using traditional heating methods.

comments.author: @krishnabhatt3377
comments.text: fascinating.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: We appreciate your feedback, but our channel is more about travel documentary topics than news or politics topics. Thank you!

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Thanks a lot for sharing the thoughts!

comments.author: @EXPLOREHORIZONS66
comments.text: Bolivia looks breathtaking, but living at such extreme altitude must be incredibly difficult. The landscapes feel both beautiful and unforgiving.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

comments.text: Why you did not mention  Cochabamba  and Santa Cruz?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: The documentary focused on the Altiplano region, which doesn't include Cochabamba or Santa Cruz.

comments.author: @Putaplanets
comments.text: I don't think I could ever cope with that thin air, massive respect to the locals but this place seems way too hostile for normal living compared to the lowlands.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It definitely takes some getting used to. The human body is amazing at adapting, but it's a challenge for sure.

comments.author: @NomadicCorner
comments.text: Bolivia is a country worth discovering

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: Absolutely! It's a country with so much to offer, and I'm glad I could share a glimpse of its beauty and challenges.

comments.author: @Earthcue
comments.text: According to some information I've read, there are 68-million-year-old dinosaur footprints on a sheer cliff in Bolivia… Has anyone else heard of this? Or has anyone actually seen them in real life?

replies.author: @njackson6115
replies.text: Yes! We saw them-a cement company found them. It’s amazing.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: That's a fascinating piece of information! I've heard whispers about ancient footprints in Bolivia, but haven't had the chance to investigate personally. It definitely adds another layer of intrigue to an already incredible country.

comments.author: @kathyxiongchannel138
comments.text: very good video share for people to see

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: I’m really glad you enjoyed it, thanks for watching! 🏔️✨

comments.author: @RJFP56
comments.text: How well does it handle its sewage treatment and water treatment plants?  I’m just asking due to such a large congested population produces a lot of waste ?  Most places look very well maintained overall .

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: That's a really insightful question about the infrastructure! While I focused more on the cultural and scenic aspects, it's definitely something crucial for such a large population.

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: Some of these places are so beautiful that it's hard to believe they haven't become famous tourist destinations already.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: It truly is amazing how many breathtaking spots remain relatively untouched by mass tourism. 🏔️✨

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Bolivia feels like Earth decided to stack mountains all the way to the clouds, then asked people, "Think you can live here?" Amazingly, they answered, "Sure." Meanwhile, I get winded carrying groceries upstairs. The resilience here is just as breathtaking as the scenery. Could you live at this altitude?

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: The resilience of the people living at such extreme altitudes is truly humbling to witness! ✨

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K

comments.author: @XPlanet-TV
comments.text: Does anyone else think Bolivia is one of the most beautiful places in South America despite all the hardships? The more I watch, the more I want to experience it for myself.

replies.author: @bottlethrower1544
replies.text: I agree after seeing this. Its wild!

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: I completely get that feeling. Bolivia has this rare mix of beauty and struggle that makes every landscape feel even more meaningful. From the endless salt flats to the high mountain cities, it almost seems impossible that people have built entire lives in such extreme conditions. Do you think actually visiting would change the way we see comfort and everyday life? 🌎🏔

replies.author: @trueunrealglobe
replies.text: The storytelling makes each location feel alive. I found myself caring about the history just as much as the incredible landscapes.

replies.author: @PlanetVista4K
replies.text: We feel the same! ❤️ Bolivia has such a unique kind of beauty. If you could visit, where would you go first?
